rulings.Case Title: SCOTT J. MANDELL ET AL VS. GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ARTHUR FONDEN's Motion To Reopen Discovery Pro Tem Judge Jason Yurasek, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to submit prior to the hearing electronically via email to Judge Pro Tem Yurasek (jyjudgeprotem@gmail.com) a discovery plan that sets forth a timeline for the service, response, and any motions to compel of discovery by category (e.g., written, testimonial, expert). The Court encourages the parties to meet and confer and submit a joint plan. Whether separate or joint, the parties should submit in detail all discovery sought to be permitted by either or both parties should the Court grant the request to reopen discovery. The parties shall also be prepared to argue prejudice, or lack thereof, for granting such requests for discovery. The parties to prepare and bring to court proposed orders. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jyjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAFEDOCS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. SWETT & CRAWFORD A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2019, Line 3, Defendant Swett & Crawford'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Defendant Swett & Crawford's motion for sanctions against plaintiffs is granted in part. Plaintiffs are required to pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,474 within fourteen days of the filing of an order on this motion and must also provide supplemental discovery responses as stated on the bottom of page 4 of the report of Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead within fourteen days of the filing of an order on this motion. The court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for those recommendations in the report of Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Swett & Crawford is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches the report of Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SHANE STEVENS VS. LLOYD BROWN, DBA LLOYD BROWN SECURITY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Protective Order Restricting Use Of Video Of Subject Incident
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2019, Line 13, PLAINTIFF SHANE STEVENS' Motion To Lift Protective Order Restricting Use Of Video Of Subject Incident Plaintiff Shane Stevens' motion to lift protective order is denied. Mr. Stevens failed to show that the video produced by Mr. Luini is not entitled to the protections in the protective order or that his ability to prosecute this case is impaired by that protective order. While some of the evidence submitted with the moving papers is suggestive that counsel for defendants may be interested in avoiding embarrassment of their clients by public dissemination of the video, reviewed as a whole, the evidence submitted on this motion shows that defendants have a well-founded good faith belief that there are protectable creative rights in the video and based on that belief they resisted production of the video without a protective order. The protective order is not an unconstitutional prior restraint on the free speech rights of Mr. Stevens or his counsel. Absent his utilization of discovery procedures, Mr. Stevens likely would not have received the video. Mr. Stevens cites no authority, nor is the court aware of any authority, that precluding use of information obtained through discovery in a civil case for purposes other than the case in which the information was obtained violates any free speech principle. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Big Billy is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

Counsel for the Bank is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and a proposed judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES, LLP VS. CHUNG W CHAN JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration, Dismiss Plaintiffs Claims, And Stay The Instant Litigation In Favor Of Arbitration. Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 8, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTs CHUNG CHAN's Motion To Compel Arbitration, Dismiss Plaintiffs Claims, And Stay The Instant Litigation In Favor Of Arbitration. Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Defendant Defendant Chung Chan, Jr.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending completion of arbitration proceedings. If Mr. Chan does not cooperate in obtaining resolution of the claims filed by plaintiff Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLC by arbitration, TSLO is given leave to file a motion for determination that Mr. Chan has waived his right to arbitration. TLSO has not satisfied its "heavy burden" that Mr. Chan has forfeited his right to arbitrate TLSO's claims. (Saint Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal, 4th 1187, 1195). TLSO has not shown how it has been prejudiced by any action or inaction of Mr. Chan. Delay alone, without more, is not a basis for a finding of waiver especially where, as here, some of the delay could reasonably be attributable to TLSO's own inaction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Chan is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
